status_values.not_started: No implementation or executable harness work exists yet.
status_values.specified: The behavior is documented but not executable yet.
status_values.in_progress: Code, tests, or harness work has started.
status_values.complete: Implementation exists and required harness coverage passes.
status_values.blocked: Progress depends on a missing design decision or dependency.
status_values.blocked_core_extension: Progress needs a generic Hermes core extension, not sync logic in core.

findings.summary: Hermes PluginManager discovers bundled, user, project, and entry-point plugins. Directory plugins need plugin.yaml and __init__.py with register(ctx).
Standalone user plugins are opt-in through plugins.enabled. The sync harness must test disabled, enabled, and missing-plugin states.
Existing hooks include post_tool_call, on_session_end, pre_llm_call, post_llm_call, on_session_start, and approval hooks.
PluginContext.register_command registers in-session slash commands; CLI and gateway dispatch plugin commands after built-ins and quick commands.
PluginContext.register_tool delegates to tools.registry.register so plugin tools can appear with built-in tools.

PluginContext.register_cli_command records commands, but hermes_cli/main.py currently wires only memory plugin CLI commands into argparse before parse. A generic plugin CLI bridge is needed for hermes sync.
Hermes persists sessions and messages in profile-aware SQLite state.db. Sync must export snapshots through sqlite3 or SessionDB APIs and must not sync state.db, WAL, or SHM files.

openai_docs.summary: OpenAI hosts a read-only Docs MCP server for developer documentation. This session did not expose that MCP resource, so official docs pages were used as fallback.
Codex reads AGENTS.md files and layers global and project instructions.
Harnesses should parse operations, validate paths, apply operations in a controlled workspace, report one result per operation, and make atomicity explicit.
Shell execution should be non-interactive, preserve failed outputs, return timeout outcomes, and keep network access tightly scoped.

required_execution_gates: run path safety checks before sync actions
prove idempotency for push, pull, and once

harness_scenarios.expected: Plugin manifest metadata is parseable and compatible with Hermes plugin discovery.
/sync status reports scan state without mutating profile data or remote data.
/sync subcommands route to the same implementation contracts as plugin tools and harness adapter calls.
sync_* tools register through the Hermes tool registry with stable schemas.
sync_status returns status data without upload, import, delete, or profile mutation beyond safe local sync metadata initialization.
Hermes core exposes generic plugin CLI-command registration before argparse parsing.
Setup creates device.json with stable per-profile identity under an isolated temporary profile.
Setup creates manifest.sqlite with the required object, revision, dirty, tombstone, and conflict fields.
Manifest rows are never created for blocked files or runtime-only paths.
Only explicit sync scopes are scanned.
Traversal, absolute escapes, and symlink escapes are rejected before file operations.
Symlinks that resolve outside temporary profile or remote roots are rejected.
Blocked patterns never enter manifest, remote objects, fixtures, traces, or docs examples.
The local-folder backend can upload, list, download, and tombstone allowed objects in a temporary remote.
Push processes outbox objects only after scan and manifest updates succeed.
Pull stages remote objects into inbox before applying user-visible imports.
Status reports no dirty objects.
Non-secret config appears in outbox.
.env and token-like keys are skipped everywhere.
state.db, WAL, and SHM files are never synced as files.
Text artifact arrives on the second isolated profile.
Logs, caches, tmp files, lock files, and watcher state remain local.
Session JSON is exported from SQLite without copying SQLite files.
Second push creates no additional remote changes.
Second pull creates no additional local changes.
Second once run makes no changes.
Deletes create tombstones and import as explicit deletes.
Concurrent text edits create deterministic conflict records and conflict files.
Latest binary file wins while preserving a conflict copy.
Allowed changes sync after one interval and runtime files stay local.
Pause and resume state is stored only on the local device and never uploaded.
A previous object version can be restored without corrupting manifest state.
All remote backends pass the same object, manifest, tombstone, conflict, and idempotency contract.
Future session-level hooks are generic Hermes plugin capabilities and contain no sync policy.

immediate_next_actions: Add dedicated config_export and secret_exclusion harness scenarios for sanitized config handling.
Add dedicated artifact_push_pull and runtime_file_exclusion harness scenarios around post-tool artifacts.
Export session snapshots from state.db through sqlite3 reads without syncing SQLite files.
Implement tombstone propagation and conflict creation on top of the local-folder backend.
Treat top-level hermes sync as blocked until Hermes core exposes generic plugin CLI registration before argparse parsing.
